articles.title: Suno leans into customization with v5.5
articles.summary: Suno released v5.5 of its AI music model with major customization features including Voices, My Taste, and Custom Models. The Voices feature allows users to train the vocal model on their own voice by uploading audio samples.

articles.title: Anthropic's Claude popularity with paying consumers is skyrocketing
articles.summary: Claude's paid subscriptions have more than doubled this year according to Anthropic. Consumer user estimates range widely from 18 million to 30 million total users.

articles.title: Elon Musk's last co-founder reportedly leaves xAI
articles.summary: Another co-founder has departed from xAI, leaving only two of the original 11 co-founders remaining. This continues the pattern of executive departures from Musk's AI company.

articles.title: Meta's legal defeat could be a victory for children, or a loss for everyone
articles.summary: Two US juries held Meta liable for hundreds of millions of dollars for harming minors, with YouTube also found liable in Los Angeles. Both companies are appealing the decisions in cases that could reshape social media accountability.

articles.title: Apple's long, bitter App Store antitrust war
articles.summary: The Stepback newsletter examines Apple's ongoing antitrust battles over App Store practices. The piece traces the evolution from Microsoft's 1998 antitrust case to Apple's current legal challenges.

articles.title: Bluesky leans into AI with Attie, an app for building custom feeds
articles.summary: Bluesky launched Attie, a new app that uses AI to help users build custom feeds on the open social networking protocol atproto. This represents Bluesky's move toward AI-powered social media features.

articles.title: Why can't TikTok identify AI generated ads when I can?
articles.summary: The article examines the lack of proper AI labeling on TikTok ads, particularly from companies like Samsung. Despite clear AI generation indicators, many ads lack proper disclosure in both labels and fine print.

articles.title: Stanford study outlines dangers of asking AI chatbots for personal advice
articles.summary: Stanford computer scientists conducted a study measuring the potential harm of AI sycophancy in chatbots. The research addresses growing concerns about the dangers of people seeking personal advice from AI systems.
